palette_hint: Deep charcoal background #1a1a1a, primary text #e8e2d5 (warm off-white), accent red #c0392b and accent amber #d4a017 used for pull quotes, rule lines, and section labels.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif at heavy weights (700–900) for editorial authority; body copy in system monospace (Courier New) at 15px/1.8 for a document-archive feel; section labels in Arial Narrow uppercase tracking 0.2em.
description: Iron Meridian Atlas is a long-form multimedia essay investigating the 1973 closure of the Harwick Steel Works in Pennsylvania — its economic aftermath, the families it fractured, and the contested memory of industrial labor in America.
constraints: Each page must contain at least 3 visually distinct layout regions: a full-bleed header band (100vw, min-height 180px), a constrained editorial column (max-width 740px centered), and at least one breakout section using CSS grid with asymmetric columns (e.g. 2fr 1fr or 3fr 2fr) that bleeds to 1100px wide.

Pull quotes must be typeset in Georgia italic at 2rem, colored #d4a017, left-bordered with a 4px solid #c0392b rule, and floated or positioned outside the main 740px column by at least 60px on desktop.
A consistent spacing system: section gaps of 80px, internal component padding of 32px, micro-spacing (captions, labels) at 12px — these values must be used verbatim throughout.

Section labels (e.g. 'FIELD NOTES', 'TESTIMONY', 'ARTIFACT') appear as uppercase Arial Narrow in #c0392b at 11px letter-spacing 0.25em, placed above headings as a visual rhythm marker on every major section.

No decorative gradients or box-shadows — visual depth comes exclusively from background color contrast between sections (#1a1a1a, #111111, #242424), border rules in #333333, and typographic weight contrast.

page_specs.threshold: The entry page establishes the essay's stakes with a full-bleed header containing the essay title 'Iron Meridian' in 96px Georgia bold against #111111, below which a three-column grid presents three dated headlines from 1971–1974 Harwick Gazette clippings (reproduced as styled blockquotes). A centered 740px editorial column opens the essay's first section — a 400-word prologue set in Courier New describing the smell of the Monongahela River on the morning of the final furnace shutdown. A breakout 2fr/1fr grid below contrasts a long caption-style annotation against a styled ASCII-art approximation of the steel plant floor plan.
page_specs.cartography: A geography-of-loss page structured around four distinct layout panels: a full-bleed section label band reading 'MAPPED TERRITORY' in red, followed by a CSS grid of six location-cards (each 280px wide, #242424 background, 32px padding) naming specific Harwick neighborhoods — Slag Hill, Coke Row, The Flats, Mill Gate, Furnace Bend, Widow's Walk — each with a 60-word description of what occupied that space before and after 1973. A breakout asymmetric 3fr/2fr region places a dense prose passage on the left against a right column of five indented sub-entries formatted like an index, listing displaced residents by decade of departure.
page_specs.evidence: An archive page presenting documentary evidence in three visually separated sections: first, a 'DOCUMENTS' region using a monospace table layout (no actual table element — achieved with CSS grid) listing 12 internal Harwick Steel memos by date, sender, and a one-sentence summary, bordered in #333333 with alternating row backgrounds #1a1a1a/#242424. Second, a 'TESTIMONY' pull-quote section with four oral-history excerpts from named former workers (Dolores Fenwick, Roy Stachowski, James Aurelio Betts, and Vera Mack Cullen) each formatted as a large indented blockquote with speaker attribution in amber. Third, a narrow 740px editorial column analyzing what the documents and testimonies contradict.
page_specs.rupture: The essay's emotional and argumentative climax, built around a full-bleed header band containing only a single sentence in 48px Georgia italic: 'By March 1974, 4,200 men had no trade left to pass on.' Below, a flex-row section breaks the page into two unequal panels (60%/40%) — the left containing a dense 600-word analytical essay passage in Courier New, the right containing a vertical stack of five numbered 'COUNTER-ARGUMENT' cards in #242424 each rebutting a pro-closure economic claim made by Harwick's parent company Meridian Industrial Holdings. A final breakout grid presents a timeline of 8 events from 1973–1989 in a single-row horizontal CSS grid with amber year-labels and red rule separators.
page_specs.aftermath: The closing page uses a three-region structure: a restrained header band with the section label 'WHAT REMAINS' and a 32px subheading listing three things that still stand in Harwick (the water tower, the union hall steps, Stachowski's tavern sign). A 740px editorial column delivers the essay's 500-word closing argument in Courier New, culminating in a final pull quote in 2.4rem Georgia italic colored #d4a017. A footer-region grid (four equal columns, 80px top margin) presents four 'further reading' citations styled as bibliographic entries in monospace — real titles including 'Deindustrializing Chicago' by Bluestone & Harrison, 'Steeltown USA' by Sherry Lee Linkon, and two fictional Harwick-specific pamphlets — each with a one-sentence annotation.
